name: Norway
description: Norway's biotech sector is concentrated in Oslo, with strong expertise in radiopharmaceuticals, diagnostics, and immunotherapy. Companies like Algeta (acquired by Bayer for $2.9B) have proven the Norwegian ecosystem can produce world-class biotech.

name: Sweden
description: Sweden is one of Europe's leading biotech hubs with a deep academic ecosystem. The Medicon Valley cluster in Lund/Malmö and the Karolinska ecosystem in Stockholm drive innovation across oncology, immunology, and diagnostics.

name: Denmark
description: Denmark is home to Novo Nordisk and a thriving biotech ecosystem. The Medicon Valley region spanning Copenhagen and Malmö is one of Europe's strongest life science clusters, with deep expertise in biologics and metabolic diseases.

name: Finland
description: Finland's biotech sector leverages the country's strength in digital health and diagnostics. Helsinki's biotech cluster benefits from close ties to world-class hospitals and the Finnish biobank network.

name: United Kingdom
description: The UK is Europe's largest biotech market, anchored by the Golden Triangle of London, Cambridge, and Oxford. The Cambridge biotech cluster alone hosts 500+ companies. The UK benefits from strong academic research, the NHS as a clinical partner, and deep capital markets.

name: Germany
description: Germany is Europe's second-largest biotech market with BioNTech, CureVac, and Evotec among its flagship companies. The Munich and Heidelberg clusters are particularly strong in oncology and mRNA technology.

name: France
description: France's biotech sector is growing rapidly with Genopole in Paris as the main hub. Strong government support through Bpifrance and the French Tech initiative has boosted the ecosystem.

name: Switzerland
description: Switzerland punches far above its weight in biotech, with Basel as a global pharmaceutical hub (Roche, Novartis). The Swiss biotech ecosystem benefits from exceptional talent, favorable tax policies, and proximity to major pharma headquarters.

name: Netherlands
description: The Netherlands is a rising biotech hub with strength in gene therapy and infectious disease. The Leiden Bio Science Park and Amsterdam Science Park anchor the ecosystem.

name: Belgium
description: Belgium is a biotech powerhouse relative to its size, with Ghent as a globally recognized hub for cell and gene therapy. UCB, Galapagos, and argenx have put Belgium on the biotech map.

name: Ireland
description: Ireland is a major biopharma manufacturing hub with 9 of the top 10 global pharma companies operating there. The Dublin biotech cluster is growing rapidly with strong government incentives.

name: Austria
description: Austria's biotech sector is centered in Vienna, with strengths in immunology and vaccine development. The LISAvienna life sciences cluster supports the local ecosystem.

name: Italy
description: Italy's biotech sector is growing with Milan as the primary hub. The country has strong expertise in rare diseases and gene therapy, partly driven by academic research at institutions like San Raffaele.

name: Spain
description: Spain's biotech ecosystem is concentrated in Barcelona and Madrid. The Barcelona Biomedical Research Park (PRBB) is one of Europe's largest research hubs.

name: United States
description: The US is the world's largest biotech market by a wide margin. Boston/Cambridge is the undisputed global capital of biotech, followed by the San Francisco Bay Area and San Diego. The US benefits from deep capital markets, the NIH funding ecosystem, and the FDA's structured regulatory pathway.

name: Canada
description: Canada's biotech sector is growing rapidly with Toronto and Montreal as the main hubs. The country benefits from strong academic institutions and government R&D incentives.

name: Japan
description: Japan is Asia's most mature biotech market with a strong regulatory framework (PMDA) and deep expertise in regenerative medicine. The country was the first to approve iPSC-derived cell therapies.

name: South Korea
description: South Korea is a global leader in biosimilar development with companies like Samsung Bioepis and Celltrion. The Songdo Bio Complex in Incheon is one of Asia's largest biotech clusters.

name: China
description: China's biotech sector has exploded over the past decade with massive government investment and a growing talent pool of returnees from US/EU pharma. Shanghai's Zhangjiang Hi-Tech Park is China's biotech capital.

name: Australia
description: Australia's biotech sector is strong in immunotherapy and infectious disease, with CSL being one of the world's largest biotech companies. Melbourne is the primary hub.

name: Singapore
description: Singapore has positioned itself as Asia's biotech gateway with the Biopolis research hub. Strong government incentives and a favorable regulatory environment attract multinational R&D centers.

name: India
description: India is the world's largest vaccine manufacturer (Serum Institute) and a growing force in biosimilars. Hyderabad's Genome Valley is India's biotech capital with 200+ companies.

name: Israel
description: Israel punches far above its weight in biotech and digital health. The country's startup culture, military technology transfer, and strong academic institutions (Weizmann Institute, Technion) drive innovation.

name: Brazil
description: Brazil's biotech sector is the largest in Latin America, with strength in vaccines and tropical disease research. Instituto Butantan in São Paulo is a major vaccine producer.
